executive_summary.one_paragraph: CLDN18 (claudin-18) is a validated, surface-accessible tight-junction tetraspanin-topology transmembrane protein with two isoforms: CLDN18.1 (lung-restricted, normal) and CLDN18.2 (gastric-restricted normally, broadly ectopically activated in cancer). Multiple live-cell flow cytometry and membranous IHC datasets confirm plasma-membrane ECD exposure. Zolbetuximab approval in gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma operationalizes this accessibility clinically. Key risks are junctional subdomain restriction in polarized epithelium, tumor-context state dependence for CLDN18.2, and CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion-related localization uncertainty in a minor gastric cancer subset.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: CLDN18 surface accessibility is supported by multiple direct methods from independent sources. (1) Live-cell flow cytometry (non-permeabilized) on N87 cells expressing CLDN18.1/18.2 with a pan-specific antibody (a1_evi_04); live-cell FACS binding assays on intact tumor cells targeting CLDN18.2 ECD (a1_evi_07, a1_evi_08); and the foundational Sahin et al. 2008 study (a1_evi_09) showing anti-CLDN18.2 mAbs bind cancer cell surfaces. (2) IHC with explicit membranous staining criteria (excluding cytoplasmic) in multiple primary human tumor cohorts: PDAC (a1_evi_14), BTC (a1_evi_17), gastric-type neoplasms (a1_evi_23). These represent two distinct direct method families (live-cell flow cytometry + membranous IHC) from multiple independent sources. The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion caveat (a1_evi_25) is restricted to a rare genomic subtype and does not meaningfully contradict surface expression of wild-type CLDN18.

antibodies.name: pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Pan-specific; recognizes both CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 isoforms. Used on ectopically expressing N87 cells.

expression_observations.context: N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2; live-cell surface staining with pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody

antibodies.name: anti-CLDN18.2 test antibodies (hVH6 nanobody series)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Counter-screened against recombinant CLDN18.1 at each development stage to rule out cross-reactivity with lung isoform.

expression_observations.context: Live target cells stained with serial dilutions of anti-CLDN18.2 antibodies; live/dead red fixable gating applied; demonstrates ECD accessibility of CLDN18.2 on intact cells
FACS on live pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma cell lines to measure antibody binding to CLDN18.2 on cell membrane surface

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody (Sahin et al. 2008 / zolbetuximab progenitor)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Shown to bind CLDN18.2 but not CLDN18.1 (lung isoform); selectivity established by counter-screening against CLDN18.1.

expression_observations.context: Cancer cell lines; monoclonal antibodies recognize CLDN18.2 antigen on surface of cancer cells; basis for zolbetuximab program

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (SPOTLIGHT/GLOW companion diagnostic)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Used as companion diagnostic for zolbetuximab; isoform specificity for CLDN18.2 assumed by trial design.

expression_observations.context: Gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma patient tumor tissue; SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trial eligibility required moderate-to-strong membranous staining in ≥75% tumor cells

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 antibody clone 43-14A
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Clone 43-14A used for IHC in PDAC cohort; scoring challenges noted for 1+ vs 2+ membranous staining.

expression_observations.context: PDAC patient tumor tissue cohort; cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in 40–75% tumor cell range; positivity defined as ≥75% moderate-to-strong membranous

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (BTC cohort, SPOTLIGHT/GLOW methodology)

expression_observations.context: Biliary tract cancer (BTC) patient cohort, n=237; CLDN18 membranous expression in 29.5% of samples; strict positivity (≥75% moderate-to-strong membranous) in 5.5%
BTC cohort; homogeneous pattern defined as moderate-to-strong membranous staining in >90% tumor area

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (membranous-only criterion)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Only membranous staining considered positive; cytoplasmic staining excluded from scoring.

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (endocervical adenocarcinoma cohort)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Cytoplasmic/membranous staining combined for positivity threshold >5%; two blinded pathologists evaluated independently.

expression_observations.context: Endocervical adenocarcinoma tumor tissue; membranous staining scored 0–5+ by % tumor cells with any membranous staining; positivity threshold >5% cytoplasmic/membranous

antibodies.name: Validated anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Source states 'validated antibodies' without further specifics.

expression_observations.context: Gastric cancer patient tumor tissue; CLDN18 positivity defined as membranous staining in ≥75% tumor cells with moderate-to-strong intensity

antibodies.name: Anti-CLDN18 IHC antibody (CD-SBN / gastric-type neoplasm cohort)

expression_observations.context: CD-SBN (stomach/biliary neoplasms) patient tissue; ~91% of CDH17-positive cases showed ≥40% extent moderate-to-strong membranous CLDN18 expression; frequently co-expressed with gastric-type mucins MUC5AC and MUC6
Cell Surface Protein Atlas mass spectrometry surfaceome data; CLDN18 identified as a surface protein used as reference gene list for integrative genomic analysis
PDAC target-evaluation table; CLDN18 listed as membrane-localized protein; expression ratio tumor/normal ~2.1; no crystal structure or druggability data noted

antibodies.name: hVH6 nanobody (CLDN18.2-targeting TAC construct)
antibodies.cross_reactivity_notes: Counter-screened against CLDN18.1 to confirm CLDN18.2 selectivity. Used as ECD-binding domain in TAC construct.

expression_observations.context: CLDN18.2-expressing tumor cells engaged by TAC-T cells via hVH6 nanobody binding to CLDN18.2 ECD; confirms surface accessibility of CLDN18.2 for therapeutic engagement

Gastric cancer / CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion context; review assertion that zolbetuximab and CAR-T therapies require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy; surface localization may be disrupted by fusion
Ovarian tumor vs. normal tissue transcriptomic analysis; CLDN18 identified as adhesion protein with extracellular antigenic epitopes showing differential expression between tumor and normal tissue

non_surface_expression.context: Ovarian tumor vs. normal tissue transcriptomic analysis identifying CLDN18 as differentially expressed adhesion protein with extracellular epitopes
IHC membranous staining of CLDN18 in biliary tract cancer cohort (237 samples); 29.5% any expression, 5.5% strict positivity
IHC membranous staining of CLDN18 in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma cohort using clone 43-14A
IHC membranous CLDN18 expression in CD-SBN gastric-type neoplasms; ~91% of CDH17-positive cases with ≥40% moderate-to-strong membranous staining
IHC membranous staining of CLDN18.2 in gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma as companion diagnostic for zolbetuximab (SPOTLIGHT/GLOW trial eligibility criterion)

therapeutic_engagement.description: Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody; Astellas) is approved for HER2-negative, CLDN18.2-positive gastric/GEJ adenocarcinoma, validated in Phase III trials SPOTLIGHT and GLOW. Clinical eligibility required IHC-confirmed membranous CLDN18.2 expression in ≥75% of tumor cells. CAR-T therapies and TAC (T-cell antigen coupler) constructs using anti-CLDN18.2 nanobodies (e.g., hVH6) are in emerging development. CLDN18.2 is also under investigation in PDAC, biliary tract cancers, and ovarian cancer contexts.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: CLDN18 is a tight-junction transmembrane protein with no documented secreted isoform. Both CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 are expressed at the cell surface as membrane-anchored proteins, confirmed by live-cell flow cytometry on non-permeabilized cells. Therapeutic agents (zolbetuximab, CAR-T, TAC constructs) engage the extracellular domain of the membrane-bound form exclusively. A caveat exists for CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion variants, where surface localization may be altered.

contradicting_evidence.claim: The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein found in a subset of gastric cancers may have altered subcellular localization relative to wild-type CLDN18.2. If the fusion protein is mislocalized away from the cell surface, therapies dependent on surface-accessible CLDN18 (e.g., zolbetuximab, CAR-T) may be ineffective in fusion-bearing tumors. This represents a caveat for assuming surface accessibility of CLDN18 in this tumor subtype.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: The ARHGAP26 fusion partner may disrupt the transmembrane topology or trafficking signals of CLDN18, potentially rerouting the fusion protein intracellularly. However, this is a speculative concern raised in a review with no direct experimental localization data for the fusion protein; wild-type CLDN18.2 surface expression in non-fusion-bearing tumors remains well-supported.

cell_types.cell_type: bile duct precursor lesion epithelial cells

anatomical_accessibility.context: polarized epithelium (gastric mucosa, lung, and other epithelial tissues — normal and tumor)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: CLDN18 is a tight-junction claudin; HPA assigns subcellular localization to cell junctions. In intact polarized epithelium the extracellular loops are embedded at the lateral tight-junction belt, physically shielded from the basolateral interstitium and apical lumen, limiting systemic antibody access.

anatomical_accessibility.context: gastric cancer and other tumor epithelia (ectopic/orthotopic CLDN18.2 expression)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: Tumor cells frequently lose tight-junction polarity, partially redistributing claudin-18 to more accessible membrane domains. IHC shows membranous staining and flow cytometry on intact cells confirms surface exposure, but residual junctional architecture in well-differentiated tumors may still limit full systemic binder access.

anatomical_accessibility.context: gastric cancer with CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein's exact subcellular localization is uncertain; fusion may disrupt normal tight-junction targeting, rendering the CLDN18 extracellular domain either more or less accessible than wild-type, and suitability for surface-targeting therapies requires further study.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Normal differentiated gastric epithelium (CLDN18.2) and normal lung epithelium (CLDN18.1)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 expression is strictly confined to differentiated gastric epithelial cells; CLDN18.1 is lung-specific. Both isoforms arise from alternative promoters, restricting surface presence to these two epithelial lineages in normal tissue.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Therapeutic binders targeting CLDN18.2 have a narrow normal-tissue on-target window limited to gastric and (for CLDN18.1) lung epithelium, reducing off-tumor toxicity risk in most other tissues.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric stem cell zone (progenitor/undifferentiated gastric epithelium)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is absent from the gastric stem cell zone but is present on differentiated gastric epithelial cells, indicating surface expression is gated by the differentiation state within the gastric lineage.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface-accessible CLDN18.2 epitopes are available only on terminally differentiated gastric cells, not on progenitors; stem cell compartment is not targeted by CLDN18.2-directed therapies.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tissue (no CLDN18.2 expression)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Malignant tumors of pancreas, esophagus, ovary, and lung
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 undergoes ectopic activation in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, where it is absent from corresponding normal tissues. Prevalence ranges broadly by tumor type and histologic subtype.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Tumor-restricted ectopic surface expression creates a therapeutic window: CLDN18.2 becomes accessible on tumor cells in organs where normal tissue lacks it, enabling tumor-selective targeting.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal gastric mucosa with CLDN18.2 restricted to differentiated epithelium
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Primary gastric cancers and their distant metastases
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 surface expression is retained upon malignant transformation in a significant proportion of gastric cancers (IHC prevalence ~54.5%) and is maintained in metastatic lesions.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Persistent membranous CLDN18.2 in gastric cancer and metastases supports continued surface accessibility for antibody-based therapies (e.g., zolbetuximab) throughout disease progression.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal lung tissue expressing CLDN18.1 (not CLDN18.2)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is expressed in 68% (27/40) of lung mucinous adenocarcinomas, representing ectopic isoform switching or activation in a histologically defined subtype.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: CLDN18.2 becomes surface-accessible in the majority of lung mucinous adenocarcinomas, expanding the targetable patient population beyond gastric cancer for CLDN18.2-directed therapies.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal bile duct epithelium (no CLDN18.2 expression)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Cholangiocarcinoma and bile duct precursor lesions (intraductal papillary neoplasm, dysplasia)
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18.2 is expressed in 52% of cholangiocarcinomas and in precursor lesions (intraductal papillary neoplasm, dysplasia), indicating early disease-state induction before frank malignancy.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Surface CLDN18.2 is detectable even at pre-malignant stages in the bile duct, broadening potential early intervention windows, though clinically relevant strong positivity (≥75% cells) is only ~5.5% of BTCs.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Polarized epithelial cells with intact tight junctions
accessibility_modulation.change: CLDN18 localizes to tight junctions at lateral cell-cell interfaces in polarized epithelium, confining surface expression to the paracellular/lateral subdomain rather than the apical or basolateral free membrane.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: In intact polarized epithelium, CLDN18 extracellular loops are sequestered at tight junctions and may be sterically occluded; disruption of polarity in tumors may expose these epitopes to circulating antibodies or effector cells.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Gastric cancer cells with wild-type CLDN18.2 at the plasma membrane tight junction
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Gastric cancer cells harboring CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion
accessibility_modulation.change: The CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein has uncertain subcellular localization; normal tight-junction targeting may be disrupted, potentially preventing surface display of the CLDN18 extracellular domain.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: If fusion mislocalizes the protein away from the plasma membrane, therapeutic modalities requiring surface CLDN18 (antibodies, CAR-T) may be ineffective in fusion-positive tumors; additional localization studies are needed.

structure.source: AlphaFold DB (STUB — fetchers not yet built)

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: No co-receptor or obligate trafficking partner has been identified in the merged ledger as required for CLDN18 plasma-membrane targeting. Claudins are autonomous tight-junction components; CLDN18 surface expression is confirmed by live-cell flow cytometry without partner co-expression requirements.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: HPA assigns CLDN18 to cell junctions; A2 notes tight-junction localization at lateral cell-cell interfaces in polarized epithelium, potentially sterically occluding ECD access to systemic antibodies. Tumor-induced polarity loss partially relieves this restriction, confirmed by live-cell FACS and IHC membranous staining in tumor cohorts, but well-differentiated tumors may retain junctional sequestration.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: Claudins are 4-pass transmembrane proteins with two short extracellular loops (EL1 ~50 aa, EL2 ~20 aa). The accessible ECD per loop is limited, yet sufficient for monoclonal antibody binding as demonstrated by zolbetuximab and nanobody programs; nanobodies exploit the compact epitope effectively.

epitope_masking.rationale: In intact tight junctions the short extracellular loops of CLDN18 face the paracellular cleft and are flanked by opposing claudin molecules on adjacent cells, creating steric conformation-dependent masking. This is inferred from junctional biology; no direct glycan-masking or cleavage data is present in the ledger. Tumor polarity disruption partially unmasks epitopes.

evidence.claim: Claudin-18 gene product is described as a member of the claudin multigene family of proteins that constitute tight junction strands in epithelial cells, establishing its classification as a tight junction transmembrane protein with cell-surface accessibility.

spans.quote: The gene product exhibits an amino acid sequence similar to those of the claudin multigene family of proteins that constitute tight junction strands in epithelial cells.

evidence.claim: HPA database annotates CLDN18 subcellular localization to Cell Junctions, consistent with tight junction membrane localization and surface accessibility at cell-cell interfaces.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Cell Junctions.

evidence.claim: A target-evaluation table for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma imaging lists CLDN18 as a membrane-localized protein, with no crystal structure or druggability data noted, supporting its classification as a surface-accessible membrane protein in PDAC.

spans.quote: crystal structure CEACAM5 Membrane 353.2 257.8 0.7 ND ND Low Yes MUCL3 Membrane/cytoplasm 152.0 57.8 0.4 ND ND ND No TMPRSS4 Membrane/intracellular 131.0 72.1 0.6 Medium Medium Low No COL17A1 Cell junction, desmosome, membrane 96.8 78.4 0.8 ND ND ND No MSLN Membrane 67.5 272.7 4.0 ND ND ND No CLDN18 Membrane 64.4 133.3 2.1 ND ND ND No PSCA Membrane 51.8 135.2 2.6 Low ND ND No AQP5 Membrane, cytoplasmic vesicle membrane 37.9 68.9 1.8 Low Low Low Yes VSIG2 Membrane 18.9 159.0 8.4 High High Medium No FXYD3 Membrane 17.4 236.8 13.6 ND Medium ND No GJB3 Membrane

evidence.claim: Live-cell flow cytometry on N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody (recognizing both isoforms) demonstrates surface expression of both CLDN18 isoforms on intact, non-permeabilized cells. Method family: flow cytometry (live-cell, surface staining); isoforms: CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2.

spans.quote: Left, Flow cytometry analysis of CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 surface expression in N87 cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms.

evidence.claim: During development of the hVH6 CLDN18.2-targeting nanobody, potential cross-reactivity with CLDN18.1 was explicitly ruled out at each stage through counter-screening against recombinant CLDN18.1, establishing isoform selectivity of the anti-CLDN18.2 binding agent. This is a key antibody/binder specificity validation step relevant to surface-engagement specificity.

spans.quote: Due to the close homology of CLDN18.2 with CLDN18.1, potential cross-reactivity with recombinant CLDN18.1 was ruled out at each stage of hVH6 development through counter-screening against recombinant CLDN18.1.

evidence.claim: The TAC (T-cell antigen coupler) construct targeting CLDN18.2 uses an N-terminal CD8α leader, hVH6 nanobody ECD-binding domain, c-myc tag, anti-CD3ε scFv, and CD4 transmembrane/cytosolic domains — demonstrating that the ECD of CLDN18.2 is accessible at the cell surface for therapeutic engagement by engineered T-cell receptors.

spans.quote: The structure of the final CLDN18.2-TAC receptor consists of an N-terminal CD8α leader peptide, hVH6, c-myc tag, anti-CD3ε scFv, and the transmembrane and cytosolic domains of CD4.

evidence.claim: Cell-binding flow cytometry assay performed on live target cells using serial dilutions of anti-CLDN18.2 test antibodies in FACS staining buffer, with fluorescent secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific Cat# A-21445, RRID:AB_2535862) and live/dead fixable red dye gating — a validated live-cell surface-binding assay demonstrating antibody accessibility to CLDN18.2 ECD on intact cells.

spans.quote: For cell binding assays, serial dilutions of test and control antibodies were incubated with target cells in FACS staining buffer, starting at a concentration of 1 mM and diluted fourfold across 10 points, followed by washing with FACS staining buffer and staining with secondary antibody (Thermo Fisher Scientific Cat# A-21445, RRID:AB_2535862) at a final concentration of 4 ug/mL along with live/dead red fixable reagent (ThermoFisher) at 1/1000 in FACS staining buffer for 1 hour at 4°C.

evidence.claim: FACS on live cells is used to directly measure antibody binding to CLDN18.2 on the cell membrane surface, validating that CLAUDIN 18.2 ECD is accessible to antibody binding on intact tumor cells — supporting live-cell flow cytometry as a core surface-expression detection method for CLDN18.2.

spans.quote: FACS allows us to directly measure the binding of antibodies to cell surface proteins, which is highly relevant to our goal of screening antibodies that specifically target CLAUDIN 18.2 on the cell membrane.

evidence.claim: Monoclonal antibodies raised against CLDN18.2 (the stomach/cancer isoform) were shown to bind selectively to CLDN18.2 but not to CLDN18.1 (lung isoform), and to recognize the antigen on the surface of cancer cells. This foundational paper (Sahin et al. 2008) establishes CLDN18.2 as a surface-accessible cancer target with highly restricted normal-tissue expression, qualifying it for antibody therapy. This represents the basis for the zolbetuximab therapeutic program (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody, Phase III approval for gastric/GEJ cancer).

spans.quote:
Most importantly, we were able to raise monoclonal antibodies that bind to CLDN18.2 but not to its lung-specific splice variant and recognize the antigen on the surface of cancer cells.

Conclusions

Its highly restricted expression pattern in normal tissues, its frequent ectopic activation in a diversity of human cancers, and the ability to specifically target this molecule at the cell surface of tumor cells qualify CLDN18.2 as a novel, highly attractive pan-cancer target for the antibody therapy of epithelial tumors.

evidence.claim: Zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2 monoclonal antibody) and CLDN18.2-targeting CAR-T cell therapies require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy, and are discussed in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion variants where surface localization may be disrupted. This clip supports the requirement for surface-accessible CLDN18.2 ECD as a prerequisite for clinical activity of zolbetuximab (anti-CLDN18.2; Astellas; Phase III approved for gastric/GEJ cancer) and emerging CAR-T programs.

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: A transcriptomic analysis of ovarian tumor vs. normal tissue identified CLDN18 (alongside EpCAM and CLDN1) as an adhesion protein with extracellular antigenic epitopes showing differential expression between tumor and normal tissue — placing CLDN18 in the category of surface-accessible membrane proteins with oncology targeting potential in ovarian cancer.

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: Clinical eligibility for zolbetuximab-based trials (SPOTLIGHT and GLOW) required IHC-confirmed CLDN18 moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of tumor cells, directly operationalizing membrane surface detection of CLDN18 by IHC as the companion diagnostic standard.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: IHC-based membranous staining scoring for CLDN18 presents interpretation challenges, with poorly described guidelines distinguishing 1+ vs. 2+ membranous staining patterns — highlighting a methodological caveat for surface-detection quantification by IHC that can affect surface-accessibility classification accuracy.

spans.quote: Another challenge with the pathologic interpretation of CLDN18 IHC is that guidelines defining 1+ vs. 2+ membranous staining are not well described.

evidence.claim: In a PDAC cohort, CLDN18 IHC using clone 43-14A showed that cases with 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in the 40–75% tumor cell range required careful consideration for scoring — providing empirical surface-detection data from IHC in pancreatic cancer tissue.

spans.quote: With CLDN18/43–14A staining in our PDAC cohort, we found that cases exhibiting 2+ or 3+ membranous staining in the 40–75 % range required more careful consideration for scoring.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring for CLDN18 membranous staining in biliary tract cancers follows the SPOTLIGHT/GLOW trial methodology: positivity defined as ≥75% tumor cells with moderate-to-strong membranous staining, with intratumoral heterogeneity assessed only in CLDN18-positive cases.

spans.quote:
Tumor positivity for CLDN18 was determined if ≥75% of tumor cells exhibited moderate-to-strong membranous staining, following the evaluation methods used in the SPOTLIGHT and GLOW trials. 10 , 11 , 18
The presence and pattern of intratumoral heterogeneity was assessed only in cases with CLDN18 positivity, as weak staining makes it difficult to assess staining patterns.

evidence.claim: Homogeneous CLDN18 membranous expression defined as moderate-to-strong staining in >90% of tumor area; used as IHC surface-scoring criterion in BTC cohort study following SPOTLIGHT/GLOW methodology.

spans.quote: The absence of intratumoral heterogeneity (i.e. homogenous pattern of expression of CLDN18) was defined as CLDN18 being expressed in >90% of the area with a moderate-to-strong membranous staining.

evidence.claim: IHC with membranous staining criterion detected CLDN18 expression in 29.5% of 237 BTC samples, with strict positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous in ≥75% cells) in 5.5% — demonstrating CLDN18 membranous surface expression in a subset of biliary tract cancers.

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: IHC scoring criteria explicitly limit positive calls to membranous staining only, excluding cytoplasmic staining — establishing that surface membrane localization is the operative detection criterion for CLDN18 in this IHC-based study.

spans.quote: Only membranous staining was considered positive.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 IHC membranous staining scored on a 0–5+ scale from 0% to ≥75% tumor cells with any membranous staining, providing a granular surface-detection scoring system for CLDN18 membrane expression in tumor tissue.

spans.quote: The percentage of tumour cells with any membranous staining for CLDN18 was scored as follows: score 0, 0%; score 1+, 1%–4%; score 2+, 5%–9%; score 3+, 10%–24%; score 4+, 25%–74%; score 5+, ≥75%.

evidence.claim: IHC positivity threshold of >5% cytoplasmic/membranous staining used for CLDN18, with two blinded pathologists evaluating expression independently — a validated IHC surface-detection methodology with structured inter-rater reliability controls.

spans.quote:
Anycytoplasmic/membranous staining of more than 5% of tumour cells was considered positive. 9 , 26 , 33
Two pathologists (N.Y. and H.Y.) independently evaluated the p16, Rb and HIK1083 expression patterns without information about other IHC and HPV‐ISH results.

evidence.claim: IHC performed using validated antibodies in this study — providing methodological anchor for surface-membrane detection of CLDN18 in tumor tissue.

spans.quote: Immunohistochemistry was performed using validated antibodies.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 positivity defined as membranous staining in ≥75% of tumor cells with moderate-to-strong intensity — the companion diagnostic IHC criterion for therapeutic eligibility (zolbetuximab), operationalizing surface membrane expression as the defining criterion for target accessibility.

spans.quote: CLDN18 positivity was defined as membranous staining in ≥ 75% of tumor cells with a moderate-to-strong intensity.

evidence.claim: IHC in a cohort of CD-SBNs (stomach/biliary neoplasms) shows ~91% of CDH17-positive cases have ≥40% extent moderate-to-strong membranous CLDN18 expression, frequently co-expressed with gastric-type mucins — demonstrating CLDN18 membranous surface expression as a common feature of gastric-type neoplasms by IHC.

spans.quote: Interestingly, we found 92% of CD-SBNs were CDH17 positive and 91% of them showed ≥ 40% extent with moderate or strong membranous expression, and approximately half of them co-expressed gastric-type mucins, such as MUC5AC and MUC6 with frequently CLDN18.

evidence.claim: Cell surface gene lists derived from mass spectrometry surfaceome data (Cell Surface Protein Atlas) were used as the reference for membrane localization in this integrative genomic analysis — providing a cross-reference to MS-based surfaceome data that includes CLDN18 as a surface protein.

spans.quote: Cell surface genes considered for analysis were taken from lists derived from predictions from mass spectrometry data (Cell Surface Protein Atlas 103 ).

evidence.claim: CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion products may have altered subcellular localization compared to wild-type CLDN18, and therapies requiring cell surface expression (zolbetuximab, CAR-T) may be ineffective if the fusion protein is mislocalized — raising a contradictory/caveat concern for surface accessibility of CLDN18 in fusion-bearing tumors.

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 (claudin-18) is expressed in lung tissue; its expression is down-regulated in T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null mouse embryo lungs, establishing lung as the primary normal-tissue expression site for the CLDN18.1 isoform.

spans.quote: Here we describe the isolation and characterization of a novel target gene, termed claudin-18, that is down-regulated in the lungs of T/ebp/Nkx2.1-null mouse embryos.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 produces two isoforms via alternative promoter usage: a lung-specific transcript (CLDN18.1) and a stomach-specific transcript (CLDN18.2), establishing tissue-restricted isoform expression across these two organ types.

spans.quote: Alternative usage of these promoters leads to production of lung and stomach-specific transcripts.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 (isoform 2) is identified as a highly selective cell lineage marker restricted to the gastrocyte lineage, qualifying it as a strictly gastric-epithelial surface molecule in normal tissue.

spans.quote:
The purpose of this study was to discover novel targets for therapeutic antibodies in solid cancer.

Experimental design

We combined data mining and wet-bench experiments to identify strictly gastrocyte lineage-specific cell surface molecules and to validate them as therapeutic antibody targets.

Results

We identified isoform 2 of the tight junction molecule claudin-18 (CLDN18.2) as a highly selective cell lineage marker.

evidence.claim: In normal tissues, CLDN18.2 expression is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa and is absent from the gastric stem cell zone, indicating a cell-state-restricted surface availability: present in differentiated gastric epithelium, absent in progenitor/stem cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: differentiated gastric epithelial cells vs. gastric stem cell zone

cell_context.activation_state: differentiated epithelial cells (absent from stem cell zone)

spans.quote: Its expression in normal tissues is strictly confined to differentiated epithelial cells of the gastric mucosa, but it is absent from the gastric stem cell zone.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 is retained upon malignant transformation and is expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and their metastases, indicating that the gastric-restricted surface expression is maintained in the tumor context.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: primary gastric cancer tumors and metastatic lesions

cell_context.tissue: gastric mucosa / lymph node / metastatic sites

spans.quote: CLDN18.2 is retained on malignant transformation and is expressed in a significant proportion of primary gastric cancers and the metastases thereof.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 shows frequent ectopic activation beyond its normal gastric-restricted expression in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, correlating with distinct histologic subtypes — representing disease-state-induced surface expression in multiple tumor types not expressing CLDN18.2 in normal tissue.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumor tissue

spans.quote: In addition to its orthotopic expression, we found frequent ectopic activation of CLDN18.2 in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung tumors, correlating with distinct histologic subtypes.

evidence.claim: IHC detection of CLDN18.2 (≥40% tumor cells, any staining intensity) across a large pan-cancer cohort shows highest prevalence in gastric (54.5%), biliary tract (21.7%), pancreatic (20.7%), and small intestinal (18.2%) cancers; overall prevalence 16.3%.

cell_context.tissue: gastric; biliary tract; pancreas; small intestine (tumor)

spans.quote: IHC detected CLDN18.2 (≥ 40% of tumor cells showing any staining intensity) in 16.3% of patients, with highest prevalence in gastric (54.5%), biliary tract (21.7%), pancreatic (20.7%), and small intestinal (18.2%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Whole transcriptome sequencing (WTS) analysis of a large pan-cancer cohort identifies CLDN18-high tumors in 13.8% of patients overall, with highest proportions in gastric (64.5%), small intestinal (40.0%), pancreatic (37.8%), and biliary tract (20.0%) cancers.

cell_context.tissue: gastric; small intestine; pancreas; biliary tract (tumor)

spans.quote: WTS analysis with optimized transcript thresholds (n = 2191) demonstrated the CLDN18-high population to be 13.8%, with highest proportions in gastric (64.5%), small intestinal (40.0%), pancreatic (37.8%), and biliary tract (20.0%) cancers.

evidence.claim: Isoform analysis of 364 cancer patients reveals CLDN18.2 predominance (mean CLDN18.2/CLDN18.1 proportion 0.945) in tumor samples, with CLDN18.1 predominance observed in only 4.9% — confirming CLDN18.2 as the dominant tumor-expressed isoform across solid cancers.

spans.quote: Isoform analysis of 364 patients revealed CLDN18.2 predominance (mean 18.2/18.1 proportion 0.945), with CLDN18.1 predominance observed in only 4.9% of patients.

evidence.claim: IHC and FISH across multiple cancer types reveals CLDN18.2 expression in 68% of lung mucinous adenocarcinomas (27/40), 52% of cholangiocarcinomas (16/31), and only 2% of colorectal adenocarcinomas (10/423).

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: lung mucinous adenocarcinoma; cholangiocarcinoma; colorectal adenocarcinoma

spans.quote:
Aims

To clarify claudin18.2 expression and its clinicopathological features in various cancers, especially in lung adenocarcinoma.

Methods

Immunohistochemistry staining and fluorescence in situ hybridisation (FISH) were performed to detect claudin18.2 expression and CLDN18 gene rearrangement in adenocarcinoma from different organs.

Results

The results showed that claudin18.2 expression was found in 68% (27 of 40) of lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, 52% (16 of 31) of cholangiocarcinoma, 2% (10 of 423) of colorectal adenocarcinoma tissue microarray

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression is detected in bile duct precursor lesions including intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct and epithelial dysplasia adjacent to cholangiocarcinoma, indicating early-stage disease expression before frank malignancy.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: bile duct precursor lesion epithelium (intraductal papillary neoplasm; dysplasia)

spans.quote: Intraductal papillary neoplasm of the bile duct and epithelial dysplasia of the adjacent bile in cholangiocarcinoma also showed claudin18.2 expression.

evidence.claim: CLDN18.2 expression is detected in cervical adenocarcinoma, including HPV-associated carcinoma and non-HPV gastric-type carcinoma; the expression appears restricted to specific subtypes (moderately differentiated gastric-type).

spans.quote:
All three cases of cervical adenocarcinoma with claudin18.2 expression were moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma including one human papillomavirus (HPV)-associated carcinoma, two non-HPV-associated and gastric-type carcinoma. CLDN18 gene rearrangement was not found in all 22 cases with high claudin18.2 expression by FISH.

Conclusions

Our results suggest claudin18.2 might be a potential biomarker for targeted therapy on lung mucinous adenocarcinoma, cholangiocarcinoma, colorectal mucinous adenocarcinoma and gastric-type cervical adenocarcinoma.

evidence.claim: In biliary tract cancers (BTCs), CLDN18 protein is detectable by IHC in 29.5% of 237 samples overall, but clinically relevant positivity (moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% tumor cells) is found in only 5.5% (13/237 BTCs).

spans.quote: Overall, CLDN18 expression was observed in 70/237 BTCs (29.5%) samples, while CLDN18 positivity (i.e. moderate-to-strong membranous expression in ≥75% of the tumor cells) was found in 13/237 BTCs (5.5%).

evidence.claim: Clinical trial eligibility for CLDN18-targeted therapy in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) requires moderate-to-strong membranous IHC expression in ≥75% of tumor cells, confirming that a meaningful subset of PDAC expresses CLDN18 at the cell surface membrane in the tumor context.

spans.quote: Eligible patients had tumors expressing CLDN18, with moderate to strong membranous expression by immunohistochemistry (IHC) in ≥75 % tumor cells[ 16 , 17 ].

evidence.claim: In a PDAC surfaceome proteomics study, CLDN18 is assigned a membrane compartment localization with a tumor-to-normal expression ratio of 2.1 (64.4 tumor vs. 133.3 normal intensity values), suggesting moderate tumor upregulation at the membrane level in PDAC.

evidence.claim: CLDN18 protein expression was examined by IHC in representative tissue sections from PDAC tumors, chronic pancreatitis (CP), normal pancreatic tissue, duodenum, and liver, providing comparative tissue-level distribution data across normal and diseased pancreatic contexts.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC tumor; chronic pancreatitis; normal pancreas; duodenum; liver

spans.quote: The protein expressions of CEACAM5, TMPRSS4, COL17A1, CLDN18, and AQP5 in representative PDAC, CP, pancreatic, duodenal, and liver tissue sections are shown in Figure 1 .

evidence.claim: Transcriptional and proteomic surfaceome investigation of PDAC reveals CLDN18 as an established therapeutic target with low or minimal expression in most normal (toxicity-prone) tissues, supporting tumor-restricted surface accessibility of CLDN18 in PDAC.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: PDAC tumor vs. normal tissue panel

spans.quote: Transcriptional and proteomic investigation of the cell surfaceome revealed established therapeutic targets ( CLDN18 , MSLN , TROP2 , MUC1 , CEACAM5 ) and discovered additional PDAC antigens ( GPRC5A , TM4SF1 ) with low or minimal expression in most toxicity-prone normal tissues.

evidence.claim: An independent study corroborates that CLDN18 is expressed almost exclusively in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues, reinforcing the tumor-restricted ectopic expression pattern of CLDN18 across these organ types.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, lung tumor tissue

spans.quote: Another study corroborated that CLDN18 was expressed almost exclusively in pancreatic, esophageal, ovarian, and lung cancer tissues ( Sahin et al., 2008 ).

evidence.claim: Comparative transcriptomic analysis identifies CLDN18 (as an adhesion protein with extracellular antigenic epitopes) among genes with the highest differential expression between ovarian tumors and normal tissues, placing CLDN18 as a tumor-enriched surface molecule in ovarian cancer.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: ovarian tumor vs. normal ovarian tissue

spans.quote: The final set of genes encoding extracellular antigenic epitopes with the highest difference between ovarian tumors and normal tissues consisted of membrane receptors (TFRC, OPRK1, EBAG9, HTR5A, GABRB1, and CD47), adhesion proteins (EpCAM, CLDN18, and CLDN1), and molecule transporters (KCNG1, KCNH2, and SLC22A11) ( Figure 1E ).

evidence.claim: HPA database assigns CLDN18 subcellular localization to cell junctions, consistent with tight junction localization at lateral cell-cell contacts. This places the protein at a junction-restricted surface subdomain rather than freely accessible apical or basolateral plasma membrane.

spans.quote: HPA subcellular locations: Cell Junctions.

evidence.claim: HPA flags CLDN18 as a junctional protein, consistent with localization at tight junctions at lateral cell-cell interfaces. This junctional membrane subdomain is a surface subdomain in polarized epithelial cells, with accessibility potentially restricted to the lateral (paracellular) face.

spans.quote: HPA flags this gene as junctional.

evidence.claim: In the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 gene fusion (found in gastric cancer), the exact subcellular localization of the fusion protein is uncertain, raising the possibility that fusion disrupts normal tight-junction surface localization and thus may compromise accessibility of the CLDN18 extracellular domain for antibody or CAR-T therapy.

spans.quote: Clinically, CLDN18 protein has been an attractive target due to CLDN18.2 targeting antibodies demonstrating efficiency in early-phase clinical trials such as the monoclonal antibody zolbetuximab 29 as well as emerging chimeric antigen receptor-T cell (CAR-T) therapies. 30 However, in the context of CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion protein, additional studies will be needed to identify the exact localisation of the fusion gene in the cell and the suitability of therapies that require cell surface expression of CLDN18 for efficacy.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry analysis of intact N87 gastric cancer cells ectopically expressing CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 with a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody confirms surface expression of both isoforms at the plasma membrane of these cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: N87 gastric cancer cell line (ectopic overexpression of CLDN18.1/18.2)

spans.quote: Left, Flow cytometry analysis of CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 surface expression in N87 cells ectopically expressing human CLDN18.1 and CLDN18.2 using a pan-specific anti-CLDN18 antibody recognizing both isoforms.

confidence_reasoning: A1 evidence grade is direct_multi_method, supported by multiple independent live-cell flow cytometry studies (a1_evi_04, a1_evi_07, a1_evi_08, a1_evi_09) and membranous IHC in primary human tumor cohorts (a1_evi_12, a1_evi_14, a1_evi_17, a1_evi_22, a1_evi_23), plus Phase III clinical validation (zolbetuximab). The single contradicting evidence entry (CLDN18-ARHGAP26 fusion, a1_evi_25) is restricted to a rare genomic subtype and is mechanistically speculative. A2 state-dependence (junctional restriction, isoform switching) is moderate but does not contradict surface accessibility in the tumor context. No secreted or shed form exists. Overall confidence is high.
